key_themes: Improving small-cap earnings growth expected to accelerate in 2026
2x leveraged daily exposure to S&P SmallCap 600
Discussion in leveraged ETF communities for small-cap rotation plays
Low AUM (~$22M) and limited mainstream investor buzz
summary: SAA (ProShares Ultra SmallCap600), a 2x leveraged ETF, is showing positive sentiment backed by impressive returns of ~81% over one year and 19% YTD as of mid-April 2026. It is trading near its 52-week high (~$32.47) amid a broader recovery in small-cap stocks, with S&P SmallCap 600 earnings turning positive and forecasts calling for strong growth in 2026. While it garners some discussion in leveraged ETF investor circles as a way to play small-cap rotation, overall social media and news volume is low given its modest $22M AUM and specialized nature.
sources: Yahoo Finance SAA quote & performance data, ProShares official site, S&P Global / Morningstar SAA pages, Reddit r/LETFs threads (multiple mentions), ~10-15 recent X posts (mostly low relevance; some crypto $SAA or unrelated SaaStock event), Articles on S&P SmallCap 600 earnings outlook
